message.content:
You are an adversarial security auditor. Audit the hc-assay project at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay for production-readiness issues in SECURITY and DATA SOVEREIGNTY.

Focus areas:
- Any code path that could send data off-box contrary to the local-first mandate (ADR-0003)
- Hardcoded secrets, credentials, or API keys in source or config
- Input validation gaps at trust boundaries (CLI args, external parsers, network)
- Dependency vulnerabilities or unpinned versions in pyproject.toml / requirements.lock
- Supply chain: lockfile integrity, hash verification, allowlist enforcement in CI
- File permissions, temp file handling, path traversal risks
- HMAC preregistration integrity: can the proof be forged or bypassed?
- Session token or auth credential handling

Read the source files carefully. Return a JSON list of findings, each with id (S-N), severity (critical/high/medium/low), file, line, title, description, poc (proof-of-concept), suggested_fix.

Only report TRUE production-readiness problems with specific file:line evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll systematically audit the hc-assay project for security and data sovereignty issues.

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
85	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
86	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
87	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
88

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
35	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast
41	
42	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
43	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
44	
45	
46	class StakesTier(Enum):
47	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
48	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52	class ReasoningRequest:
53	    prompt: str
54	    tier: StakesTier
55	    purpose: str
56	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57	
58	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
59	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
60	
61	
62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Errors
64	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
66	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
67	
68	
69	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
70	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
71	
72	
73	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
74	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
81	    val = os.environ.get(name)
82	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
83	
84	
85	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
86	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
87	
88	
89	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
106	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
107	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
108	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
109	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
110	
111	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
112	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
113	
114	
115	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
117	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
119	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
120	
121	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
122	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
123	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
124	    """
125	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
126	
127	
128	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
129	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
130	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
131	    {
132	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
133	        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
134	        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
135	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
136	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
137	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
138	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
139	    }
140	)
141	
142	
143	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
144	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
145	
146	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
147	    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
148	    deliberately NOT matched.
149	    """
150	    ku = key.upper()
151	    return (
152	        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
153	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
154	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
155	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
156	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
157	    )
158	
159	
160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env
175	
176	
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )
205	        _inflight += 1
206	    try:
207	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
208	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
209	        with _inflight_lock:
210	            _inflight -= 1
211	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
212	    except BaseException:
213	        with _inflight_lock:
214	            _inflight -= 1
215	        raise
216	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
217	    return future
218	
219	
220	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
221	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
222	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
223	    try:
224	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
225	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
226	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
227	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
228	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
229	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
230	
231	
232	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
234	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
236	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
237	
238	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
239	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
240	    """
241	    start = None
242	    opener = closer = ""
243	    depth = 0
244	    in_string = False
245	    escape = False
246	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
247	        if start is None:
248	            if ch in "{[":
249	                start, opener = i, ch
250	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
251	                depth = 1
252	            continue
253	        if in_string:
254	            if escape:
255	                escape = False
256	            elif ch == "\\":
257	                escape = True
258	            elif ch == '"':
259	                in_string = False
260	            continue
261	        if ch == '"':
262	            in_string = True
263	        elif ch == opener:
264	            depth += 1
265	        elif ch == closer:
266	            depth -= 1
267	            if depth == 0:
268	                return text[start : i + 1]
269	    return None
270	
271	
272	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
273	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
274	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
275	    if span is None:
276	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
277	    try:
278	        return json.loads(span)
279	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
280	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
281	
282	
283	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
285	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	def _bulk_complete(
287	    prompt: str,
288	    system: str | None,
289	    temperature: float,
290	    model: str,
291	    json_mode: bool = False,
292	    *,
293	    seed: int | None = None,
294	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
295	) -> str:
296	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
297	
298	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
299	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
300	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
301	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
302	    """
303	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
304	    try:
305	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
306	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
307	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
308	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
309	        ) from exc
310	
311	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
312	        model=model,
313	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
314	        temperature=temperature,
315	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
316	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
317	    )
318	    if seed is not None:
319	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
320	    if json_schema is not None:
321	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
322	    elif json_mode:
323	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
324	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
325	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
326	    if system:
327	        messages.append(("system", system))
328	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
329	    try:
330	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
331	    except Exception as exc:
332	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
333	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
334	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
335	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
336	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
337	        )
338	        msg = str(exc).lower()
339	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
340	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
341	        )
342	        if permanent:
343	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
344	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
345	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
346	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
347	    text = text.strip()
348	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
349	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
350	    return text
351	
352	
353	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
354	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
355	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
356	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
357	        return True
358	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
359	
360	
361	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
362	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
363	    try:
364	        import anyio
365	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
366	            AssistantMessage,
367	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
368	            ResultMessage,
369	            TextBlock,
370	            query,
371	        )
372	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
373	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
374	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
375	        ) from exc
376	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
377	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
378	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
379	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
380	        )
381	
382	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
383	        system_prompt=system,
384	        model=model,
385	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
386	        setting_sources=[],
387	        allowed_tools=[],
388	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
389	    )
390	
391	    parts: list[str] = []
392	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
393	
394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        #
399	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
400	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
401	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
402	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
403	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
404	        nonlocal result_error
405	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
406	            await _drain_stream()
407	
408	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
409	        nonlocal result_error
410	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
411	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
412	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
413	                if rl == "rejected":
414	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
415	                continue
416	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
417	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
418	                continue
419	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
420	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
421	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
422	                        parts.append(block.text)
423	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
424	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
425	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
426	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
427	                ):
428	                    result_error = {
429	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
430	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
431	                    }
432	
433	    try:
434	        anyio.run(_drain)
435	    except Exception as exc:
436	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
437	
438	    if result_error is not None:
439	        status = result_error.get("status")
440	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
441	        if status in (429, 529):
442	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
443	        if status in (401, 403):
444	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
445	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
446	
447	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
448	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
449	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
450	    return text
451	
452	
453	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
454	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
455	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
456	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
457	    p = request.params
458	    system = p.get("system")
459	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
460	    model = p.get("model")
461	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
462	    seed = p.get("_seed")
463	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
464	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
465	        return cast(
466	            str,
467	            _with_timeout(
468	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
469	                    request.prompt,
470	                    system,
471	                    temperature,
472	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
473	                    json_mode,
474	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
475	                    json_schema=json_schema,
476	                ),
477	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
478	                "bulk tier",
479	            ),
480	        )
481	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
482	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
483	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
484	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
485	        llm_attrs = {
486	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
487	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
488	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
489	        }
490	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
491	            return cast(
492	                str,
493	                _with_timeout(
494	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
495	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
496	                    ),
497	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
498	                    "high-stakes tier",
499	                ),
500	            )
501	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
502	
503	
504	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
505	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
506	
507	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
508	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
509	    """
510	    if deadline is not None:
511	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
512	        if remaining <= 0:
513	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
514	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
515	    time.sleep(secs)
516	
517	
518	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
519	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
520	
521	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds the cumulative backoff so the retry budgets cannot
522	    block a caller past its overall deadline (#102).
523	    """
524	    transient = 0
525	    rate = 0
526	    while True:
527	        try:
528	            return _attempt(request)
529	        except PermanentReasoningError:
530	            raise
531	        except RateLimitError:
532	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
533	                raise
534	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
535	            rate += 1
536	        except ReasoningError:
537	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
538	                raise
539	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
540	            transient += 1
541	
542	
543	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
544	# Public seam
545	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
546	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
547	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
548	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
549	
550	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
551	
552	
553	@contextmanager
554	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
555	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
556	
557	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
558	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
559	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
560	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
561	    """
562	    try:
563	        from opentelemetry import trace
564	    except ImportError:
565	        yield
566	        return
567	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
568	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
569	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
570	        for k, v in attributes.items():
571	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
572	        yield
573	
574	
575	class TieredReasoningSeam:
576	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
577	
578	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
579	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
580	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
581	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
582	    """
583	
584	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
585	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
586	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
587	            return _run_with_retries(request)
588	
589	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
590	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
591	
592	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
593	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
594	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
595	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
596	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
597	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
598	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
599	        """
600	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
601	        system = request.params.get("system")
602	        json_system = (
603	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
604	            if system
605	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
606	        )
607	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
608	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
609	        with _span(
610	            "reasoning.run_json",
611	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
612	        ):
613	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
614	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
615	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
616	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
617	                params = dict(request.params)
618	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
619	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
620	                    prompt=request.prompt,
621	                    tier=request.tier,
622	                    purpose=request.purpose,
623	                    params=params,
624	                )
625	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
626	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
627	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
628	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
629	                try:
630	                    return extract_json(text)
631	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
632	                    last = exc
633	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
634	
635	    @staticmethod
636	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
637	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
638	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
639	            return False
640	        try:
641	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
642	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
643	                import urllib.request
644	
645	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
646	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
647	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
648	                import shutil
649	
650	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
651	        except Exception:
652	            return False
653	        return False
654	
655	
656	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
657	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
658	
659	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
660	        raise NotImplementedError(
661	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
662	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
663	        )

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217

1	"""Pre-registration integrity (METHODOLOGY.md §3, GOVERNANCE.md §2).
2	
3	GOVERNANCE §2 defines pre-registration as three steps: a hypothesis is (1) made specific and
4	typed, (2) **content-hashed and committed**, and (3) **timestamped against a trusted authority**
5	— *before* any confirmatory test. Steps 2–3 were a presence sentinel until now: ``Hypothesis.locked``
6	asserted only that ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` were non-``None``, so a hand-written
7	``timestamp_proof="rfc3161:demo"`` "locked" a hypothesis and nothing stopped a study from
8	locking *after* it had seen the baseline, or from swapping the hypothesis content after locking
9	(audit pass 1, issue #6). Both firewalls (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) and all of
10	:mod:`confirm` rest on the lock, so a sentinel lock made the three verdicts decorative.
11	
12	This module makes the lock mean what the method says, by construction:
13	
14	- **Content binding** — :func:`canonical_hypothesis_digest` is a type-faithful SHA-256 over a
15	  hypothesis's *decision-bearing* fields (statement, kind, origin, test, decision rule,
16	  predicted direction, source claim, params). A valid proof must attest to *this* digest, so
17	  the content the verdict is about is provably the content that was locked: change the claim,
18	  the test, or the decision rule after locking and verification fails.
19	- **Verifiable timestamp** — a pluggable :class:`TimestampAuthority` returns a
20	  :class:`VerifiedTimestamp` only if the proof genuinely binds the digest. The engine ships one
21	  real, data-sovereign authority (:class:`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the *Protocol*
22	  so a study can plug an RFC-3161 TSA. It ships **no** silent-accept authority.
23	- **Lock before confirm** — :func:`require_preregistered` rejects a lock whose attested time is
24	  not strictly before the confirmation moment, so a hypothesis cannot be "pre"-registered after
25	  the result is known.
26	
27	Honest scope (the lesson carried from ADR-0008): :class:`LocalHmacAuthority` proves content
28	integrity, time binding, and unforgeability *relative to a secret that lives on this box*.
29	Whoever holds that secret could backdate a proof — it is local tamper-evidence, **not**
30	third-party non-repudiation. A study that needs non-repudiation wires an RFC-3161 authority
31	(which sends only the digest, never the data, so it stays compatible with data sovereignty —
32	ADR-0003 — at the cost of an external dependency the study opts into). And content binding
33	covers the *structured fields*: free text inside ``statement``/``params`` is hashed (so it
34	cannot change after locking) but the engine cannot judge whether that text is *meaningful* —
35	that remains a review obligation (GOVERNANCE §2 "timestamped adaptive design").
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import datetime as _dt
41	import hmac
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
43	from typing import Protocol
44	
45	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json, hash_text
46	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import FirewallViolation
47	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis
48	
49	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
50	_PROOF_PREFIX = "local-hmac:v1:"
51	_PROOF_FIELD_SEP = "|"  # ISO-8601 and lowercase hex never contain "|"
52	
53	
54	class PreRegistrationError(FirewallViolation):
55	    """A hypothesis failed pre-registration verification (METHODOLOGY §3, GOVERNANCE §2).
56	
57	    A subclass of :class:`FirewallViolation` so existing ``except FirewallViolation`` handlers
58	    in the runners and studies treat a bad lock as the firewall breach it is.
59	    """
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
63	class VerifiedTimestamp:
64	    """The authority's attestation that ``digest`` existed at ``instant``."""
65	
66	    instant: _dt.datetime  # timezone-aware, normalized to UTC
67	    authority: str
68	    digest: str
69	
70	
71	class TimestampAuthority(Protocol):
72	    """Engine contract: verify that ``proof`` binds ``digest``, returning the attested time.
73	
74	    Implementations MUST raise :class:`PreRegistrationError` (not return a sentinel) when the
75	    proof is malformed, does not bind ``digest``, or is otherwise invalid — the engine treats a
76	    non-raising return as a genuine attestation.
77	    """
78	
79	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp: ...
80	
81	
82	class StampingAuthority(TimestampAuthority, Protocol):
83	    """A study-side authority that can also *issue* proofs (used by :func:`lock_hypothesis`)."""
84	
85	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str: ...
86	
87	
88	def _require_aware_utc(instant: _dt.datetime, what: str) -> _dt.datetime:
89	    if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
90	        raise PreRegistrationError(
91	            f"{what} must be timezone-aware (a naive datetime has no defined instant)"
92	        )
93	    return instant.astimezone(_UTC)
94	
95	
96	def canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> str:
97	    """A type-faithful SHA-256 over a hypothesis's decision-bearing content **and its id**.
98	
99	    Includes ``hypothesis_id``: although it is a label, it is fixed at lock time and is the key
100	    the runners attribute verdicts by (``verdict.hypothesis_id``), so leaving it unbound would
101	    let a post-lock id swap file a verdict under an id the proof never attested (adversarial
102	    review, #80). Excludes only the lock fields themselves (``locked_at``/``timestamp_proof`` —
103	    they attest *to* this digest, so binding them would be circular).
104	
105	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
106	    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
107	    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
108	    """
109	    payload = {
110	        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
111	        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
112	        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
113	        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
114	        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
115	        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
116	        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
117	        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
118	        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
119	    }
120	    try:
121	        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
122	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
123	        raise PreRegistrationError(
124	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
125	            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
126	            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
127	        ) from None
128	
129	
130	class LocalHmacAuthority:
131	    """A real, on-box timestamp authority binding a digest and an instant under a secret key.
132	
133	    ``stamp`` issues ``local-hmac:v1:<instant-iso>|<hmac-hex>`` where the MAC is
134	    ``HMAC-SHA256(secret, digest || instant)``. ``verify`` recomputes the MAC over the digest
135	    the engine derived from the *current* hypothesis and the instant carried in the proof, and
136	    constant-time compares. The proof therefore verifies iff the content is unchanged, the
137	    instant is unchanged, and the same secret is used — without the secret it cannot be forged
138	    for arbitrary content or time.
139	
140	    Honest scope: this is local tamper-evidence, not third-party non-repudiation — the holder of
141	    ``secret`` could backdate (see module docstring). Use an RFC-3161 authority when you need a
142	    third party to vouch for the time.
143	    """
144	
145	    authority_id = "local-hmac"
146	
147	    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
148	        if not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16:
149	            raise ValueError("LocalHmacAuthority secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
150	        self._secret = bytes(secret)
151	
152	    def _mac(self, digest: str, instant_iso: str) -> str:
153	        msg = canonical_json(["local-hmac:v1", digest, instant_iso]).encode("utf-8")
154	        return hmac.new(self._secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
155	
156	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str:
157	        when = instant if instant is not None else _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
158	        when = _require_aware_utc(when, "stamp instant")
159	        instant_iso = when.isoformat()
160	        return f"{_PROOF_PREFIX}{instant_iso}{_PROOF_FIELD_SEP}{self._mac(digest, instant_iso)}"
161	
162	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
163	        if not proof.startswith(_PROOF_PREFIX):
164	            raise PreRegistrationError(
165	                f"proof is not a {self.authority_id} v1 token (wrong prefix)"
166	            )
167	        body = proof[len(_PROOF_PREFIX) :]
168	        instant_iso, sep, mac_hex = body.partition(_PROOF_FIELD_SEP)
169	        if not sep or not instant_iso or not mac_hex:
170	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (expected <instant>|<mac>)")
171	        # The MAC field must be exactly the hex of a SHA-256 digest. Validating its shape here
172	        # (a) rejects garbage early and (b) keeps non-ASCII/non-hex out of hmac.compare_digest,
173	        # which raises TypeError on non-ASCII str rather than returning False (#81).
174	        if len(mac_hex) != 64 or any(c not in "0123456789abcdef" for c in mac_hex):
175	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (mac is not 64 lowercase hex)")
176	        try:
177	            parsed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(instant_iso)
178	        except ValueError:
179	            raise PreRegistrationError(f"proof instant is not ISO-8601: {instant_iso!r}") from None
180	        instant = _require_aware_utc(parsed, "proof instant")
181	        expected = self._mac(digest, instant_iso)
182	        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, mac_hex):
183	            # Either the content (digest) changed since locking, the instant was tampered, or the
184	            # proof was issued under a different secret. All are pre-registration failures.
185	            raise PreRegistrationError(
186	                "proof does not bind this hypothesis's content at the stated time "
187	                "(content changed after locking, or proof was forged/issued by another authority)"
188	            )
189	        return VerifiedTimestamp(instant=instant, authority=self.authority_id, digest=digest)
190	
191	
192	def lock_hypothesis(
193	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
194	    *,
195	    authority: StampingAuthority,
196	    instant: _dt.datetime | None = None,
197	) -> Hypothesis:
198	    """Return a locked copy of ``hypothesis`` whose proof binds its current content.
199	
200	    The engine's blessed way to pre-register: it computes the content digest, has ``authority``
201	    stamp it, and records the attested instant in ``locked_at`` so the displayed lock time and
202	    the cryptographic proof always agree. Re-locking an already-locked hypothesis is refused.
203	
204	    Scope of the seal (same honest boundary as ADR-0008): this refuses re-locking *this object*.
205	    It cannot stop a study that willfully reconstructs a hypothesis — e.g. ``dataclasses.replace``
206	    to clear the lock fields, then re-lock different content — any more than the runners can stop
207	    a builder that reflects into their frame; both need process isolation, which is out of scope.
208	    What the engine *does* guarantee is that whatever is ultimately confirmed has a proof binding
209	    its content at an attested time before confirmation. So a re-locked copy is simply a new
210	    pre-registration with a new, later timestamp — visible as such, not a silent override.
211	    """
212	    if hypothesis.locked:
213	        raise PreRegistrationError(
214	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is already locked; re-locking is forbidden"
215	        )
216	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
217	    proof = authority.stamp(digest, instant=instant)
218	    verified = authority.verify(digest, proof)  # fail loud here if the authority is misbehaving
219	    return replace(hypothesis, locked_at=verified.instant.isoformat(), timestamp_proof=proof)
220	
221	
222	def verify_preregistration(
223	    hypothesis: Hypothesis, *, authority: TimestampAuthority
224	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
225	    """Verify a hypothesis is genuinely pre-registered: present, content-bound, time-attested.
226	
227	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the hypothesis is not locked, the proof does not
228	    bind the *current* content digest, the attested instant is not a timezone-aware time, or the
229	    self-reported ``locked_at`` disagrees with the authority's attested instant.
230	    """
231	    if not hypothesis.locked:
232	        raise PreRegistrationError(
233	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
234	            "before pre-registration (METHODOLOGY §3 / GOVERNANCE §2)"
235	        )
236	    assert hypothesis.timestamp_proof is not None  # .locked guarantees this; for the type checker
237	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
238	    verified = authority.verify(digest, hypothesis.timestamp_proof)
239	    if verified.digest != digest:
240	        raise PreRegistrationError(
241	            f"authority attested digest {verified.digest!r} but the hypothesis content hashes to "
242	            f"{digest!r} — the proof does not cover this hypothesis"
243	        )
244	    # The displayed lock time must match the attested one, so a study cannot show a time the
245	    # authority did not vouch for.
246	    try:
247	        claimed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(hypothesis.locked_at or "")
248	    except ValueError:
249	        raise PreRegistrationError(
250	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} is not an ISO-8601 timestamp"
251	        ) from None
252	    if _require_aware_utc(claimed, "locked_at") != verified.instant:
253	        raise PreRegistrationError(
254	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} disagrees with the authority-attested instant "
255	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}"
256	        )
257	    return verified
258	
259	
260	def require_preregistered(
261	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
262	    *,
263	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
264	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
265	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
266	    """Gate a confirmatory step: the hypothesis must be verifiably locked *before* ``not_after``.
267	
268	    ``not_after`` is the confirmation moment (a runner captures it at entry). A lock whose
269	    attested instant is not strictly earlier is rejected — you cannot pre-register a hypothesis
270	    after the result that would confirm it is already in hand.
271	    """
272	    not_after = _require_aware_utc(not_after, "not_after")
273	    verified = verify_preregistration(hypothesis, authority=authority)
274	    if not verified.instant < not_after:
275	        raise PreRegistrationError(
276	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} was locked at "
277	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}, not strictly before the confirmation moment "
278	            f"{not_after.isoformat()!r} — a lock must precede confirmation (GOVERNANCE §2)"
279	        )
280	    return verified
281

1	"""The two firewalls, enforced as runtime primitives (ADR-0005, METHODOLOGY.md §2).
2	
3	Both separations are enforced in code and exercised by tests — never asserted only in prose.
4	
5	Firewall A — claim-blindness:
6	    The baseline must be built blind to external claims. The *structural* guarantee is that
7	    the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` (its ``build`` signature
8	    takes only a ``Corpus``) and that ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside
9	    adjudication mode. On top of that, :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` gives the claims source
10	    *custody*: hand the source to the guard, and the only way to obtain it is
11	    ``release()``/``access()``, which raise while the guard is sealed. Baseline construction
12	    runs inside ``sealed()``; adjudication runs after it.
13	
14	    Caveat (audit pass 1, issue #1): the guard cannot police a baseline builder that ignores
15	    it and reads external judgments smuggled into ``Corpus`` metadata/relations — that path
16	    is closed by the adapter contract (see schema.py), not by this guard. The guard is
17	    custodial defense-in-depth over the type-level separation, not the sole barrier.
18	
19	Firewall B — discover/confirm separation:
20	    The data used to discover a hypothesis must not be the data used to confirm it.
21	    :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit` holds disjoint, non-empty discovery and confirmation id
22	    sets and refuses to confirm on any id the discovery phase touched (or on an empty set).
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	from contextlib import contextmanager
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Generic, Iterable, Iterator, TypeVar
30	
31	C = TypeVar("C")
32	
33	
34	class FirewallViolation(RuntimeError):
35	    """Raised when a methodological firewall is breached at runtime."""
36	
37	
38	class ClaimBlindGuard(Generic[C]):
39	    """Firewall A. While sealed, the held claims source is unreachable.
40	
41	    Custodial usage (preferred) — the guard owns the source, so the baseline pipeline holds
42	    the guard rather than the source and cannot read claims while sealed::
43	
44	        guard = ClaimBlindGuard(claims_source)
45	        with guard.sealed():
46	            corpus = parser.parse(source)            # baseline build — claims unreachable
47	            baseline = build_baseline(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
48	        claims = guard.release()                      # adjudication — guard released
49	
50	    ``access(obj)`` remains for defensive checks at call sites that hold a reference directly:
51	    it returns ``obj`` when unsealed and raises while sealed.
52	    """
53	
54	    def __init__(self, claims_source: C | None = None) -> None:
55	        self._sealed = False
56	        self._claims_source = claims_source
57	
58	    @property
59	    def is_sealed(self) -> bool:
60	        return self._sealed
61	
62	    @contextmanager
63	    def sealed(self) -> Iterator["ClaimBlindGuard[C]"]:
64	        prior = self._sealed
65	        self._sealed = True
66	        try:
67	            yield self
68	        finally:
69	            self._sealed = prior
70	
71	    def release(self) -> C:
72	        """Return the custodial claims source, or raise while sealed / if none was given."""
73	        if self._sealed:
74	            raise FirewallViolation(
75	                "Firewall A: external claims released during blind baseline construction"
76	            )
77	        if self._claims_source is None:
78	            raise FirewallViolation("Firewall A: no claims source is under this guard's custody")
79	        return self._claims_source
80	
81	    def access(self, claims_source: C) -> C:
82	        """Return ``claims_source`` only if the guard is not sealed; else raise."""
83	        if self._sealed:
84	            raise FirewallViolation(
85	                "Firewall A: external claims accessed during blind baseline construction"
86	            )
87	        return claims_source
88	
89	
90	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
91	class DiscoverConfirmSplit:
92	    """Firewall B. Disjoint discovery / confirmation id partitions for unit-level claims."""
93	
94	    discovery_ids: frozenset[str]
95	    confirm_ids: frozenset[str]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        # Coerce to frozenset so the raw constructor (public API) cannot yield a "frozen" record
99	        # backed by mutable sets — otherwise the disjointness invariant below could be defeated by
100	        # post-construction in-place mutation (s.confirm_ids.add(...)) (#135).
101	        if not isinstance(self.discovery_ids, frozenset):
102	            object.__setattr__(self, "discovery_ids", frozenset(self.discovery_ids))
103	        if not isinstance(self.confirm_ids, frozenset):
104	            object.__setattr__(self, "confirm_ids", frozenset(self.confirm_ids))
105	        overlap = self.discovery_ids & self.confirm_ids
106	        if overlap:
107	            raise FirewallViolation(
108	                f"Firewall B: discovery and confirmation partitions overlap on "
109	                f"{len(overlap)} id(s), e.g. {sorted(overlap)[:3]}"
110	            )
111	        # An empty confirmation partition means there is nothing held out to confirm on; a
112	        # unit-level held-out split with no held-out object is invalid by construction. The
113	        # genuine no-holdout case must use the whole-corpus null path (audit pass 1, issue #5).
114	        if not self.confirm_ids:
115	            raise FirewallViolation(
116	                "Firewall B: confirmation partition is empty — a unit-level held-out split "
117	                "must hold out at least one object (use whole-corpus confirmation otherwise)"
118	            )
119	
120	    @classmethod
121	    def from_partition(
122	        cls, discovery_ids: Iterable[str], confirm_ids: Iterable[str]
123	    ) -> "DiscoverConfirmSplit":
124	        return cls(frozenset(discovery_ids), frozenset(confirm_ids))
125	
126	    def assert_confirm_only(self, ids: Iterable[str]) -> None:
127	        """Raise if ``ids`` is empty or any of it was seen during discovery (double-dip)."""
128	        ids = frozenset(ids)
129	        if not ids:
130	            raise FirewallViolation(
131	                "Firewall B: confirmatory test evaluated zero held-out objects — a verdict "
132	                "cannot be produced from an empty confirmation set (audit pass 1, issue #5)"
133	            )
134	        leaked = ids & self.discovery_ids
135	        if leaked:
136	            raise FirewallViolation(
137	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test touched {len(leaked)} discovery id(s) — "
138	                f"double-dipping, e.g. {sorted(leaked)[:3]}"
139	            )
140	        unknown = ids - self.confirm_ids
141	        if unknown:
142	            raise FirewallViolation(
143	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test used {len(unknown)} id(s) outside the "
144	                f"confirmation partition, e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}"
145	            )
146

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``user@evil.com:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
