81	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
82	class GateReview:
83	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
84	
85	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
86	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
87	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
88	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
89	    """
90	
91	    gate: str
92	    frm: Phase
93	    to: Phase
94	    summary: str
95	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
99	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
100	
101	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
102	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
103	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))
104	
105	
106	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
107	
108	
109	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
110	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137).
111	
112	    The source's self-reported ``claim_fingerprint()`` is an unverified self-report — it can say
113	    anything. The engine therefore computes its own type-faithful hash over the materialized
114	    records (in scored order) and records THAT as the authoritative fingerprint, so an auditor
115	    re-deriving the fingerprint from the recorded/scored claims gets the value the trail records.
116	    """
117	    return hash_value(
118	        [
119	            {
120	                "claim_id": c.claim_id,
121	                "subject": c.subject,
122	                "referents": list(c.referents),
123	                "assertion": c.assertion,
124	                "provenance": c.provenance,
125	            }
126	            for c in claim_records
127	        ]
128	    )
129	
130	
131	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
132	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
133	
134	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
135	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
136	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
137	    """
138	    seen: set[str] = set()
139	    dupes: list[str] = []
140	    for i in ids:
141	        if i in seen:
142	            dupes.append(i)
143	        seen.add(i)
144	    if dupes:
145	        raise FirewallViolation(
146	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
147	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
148	        )
149	
150	
151	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
152	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
153	
154	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
155	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
156	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
157	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
158	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
159	    """
160	    return GateDecision(
161	        approved=True,
162	        gate=review.gate,
163	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
164	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
165	    )
166	
167	
168	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
169	class StudyPlan:
170	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
171	
172	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
173	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
174	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
175	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
176	    definition).
177	    """
178	
179	    definition: StudyDefinition
180	    source: Path
181	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
182	    authority: TimestampAuthority
183	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
184	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
185	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
186	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
187	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
188	
189	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
190	        modes = self.definition.modes
191	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
192	            missing = [
193	                n
194	                for n, v in (
195	                    ("split", self.split),
196	                    ("discover", self.discover),
197	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
198	                )
199	                if v is None
200	            ]
201	            if missing:
202	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
203	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
204	            missing = [
205	                n
206	                for n, v in (
207	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
208	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
209	                )
210	                if v is None
211	            ]
212	            if missing:
213	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
214	
215	
216	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
217	class StudyResult:
218	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
219	
220	    study: str
221	    corpus_fingerprint: str
222	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
223	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
224	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
225	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
226	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
227	    source_version: str | None = None
228	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
229	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
230	
231	
232	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
233	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
234	
235	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
236	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
237	
238	    return _log
239	
240	
241	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
242	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
243	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
244	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
245	
246	
247	def run_study(
248	    plan: StudyPlan,
249	    *,
250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
253	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
254	    clock: Clock | None = None,
255	    secret: bytes | None = None,
256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
275	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
276	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
277	    defn = plan.definition
278	    modes = defn.modes
279	    required = required_phases(modes)
280	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
281	    visited: list[Phase] = []
282	    run_id: str | None = None
283	
284	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
285	        visited.append(phase)
286	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
287	
288	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
289	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
290	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
291	            return
292	        try:
293	            fn(tracker, run_id)
294	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
295	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
296	
297	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
298	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
299	        decision = gate_handler(review)
300	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
301	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
302	        trail.record(
303	            "gate",
304	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
305	            gate=review.gate,
306	            approved=approved,
307	            reason=reason,
308	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
309	        )
310	        if not approved:
311	            raise GateError(
312	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
313	            )
314	
315	    trail.record(
316	        "run_start",
317	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
318	        study=defn.name,
319	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
320	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
321	    )
322	    if tracker is not None:
323	        try:
324	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
325	                defn.name,
326	                {
327	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
328	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
329	                },
330	            )
331	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
332	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
333	
334	    ok = False
335	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
336	    try:
337	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
338	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
339	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
340	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
341	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
342	        bootstrap_tracing()
343	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
344	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
345	        # ---- INGEST ----
346	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
347	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
348	            try:
349	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
350	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
351	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
352	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
353	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
354	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
355	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
356	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
357	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
358	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
359	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
360	                raise IngestionError(
361	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
362	                )
363	            if not corpus.units:
364	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
365	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
366	            source_version: str | None = None
367	            if versioner is not None:
368	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
369	            trail.record(
370	                "ingest",
371	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
372	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
373	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
374	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
375	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
376	                source_version=source_version,
377	            )
378	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
379	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
380	                assert plan.split is not None
381	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
382	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
383	                if unknown:
384	                    raise FirewallViolation(
385	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
386	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
387	                    )
388	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
389	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
390	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
391	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
392	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
393	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
394	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
395	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
396	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
397	                    raise FirewallViolation(
398	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
399	                    )
400	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
401	                if ghost:
402	                    raise FirewallViolation(
403	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
404	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
405	                    )
406	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
407	                trail.record(
408	                    "features",
409	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
410	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
411	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
412	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
413	                )
414	
415	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
416	        baseline_instant = _now()
417	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
418	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
419	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
420	            with guard.sealed():
421	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
422	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
423	                raise FirewallViolation(
424	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
425	                    "was not built from this corpus"
426	                )
427	            trail.record(
428	                "baseline",
429	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
430	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
431	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
432	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
433	            )
434	
435	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
436	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
437	
438	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
439	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
440	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
441	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
442	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
443	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
444	
445	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
446	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
447	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
448	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
449	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
450	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
451	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
452	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
453	                trail.record(
454	                    "discovery",
455	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
456	                    n=len(hypotheses),
457	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
458	                )
459	
460	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
461	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
462	                if not held_out.units:
463	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
464	                preregister_instant = _now()
465	                for h in hypotheses:
466	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
467	                        raise FirewallViolation(
468	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
469	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
470	                        )
471	                    vt = require_preregistered(
472	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
473	                    )
474	                    trail.record(
475	                        "preregister",
476	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
477	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
478	                        digest=vt.digest,
479	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
480	                    )
481	                run_gate(
482	                    GateReview(
483	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
484	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
485	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
486	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
487	                        payload={
488	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
489	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
490	                        },
491	                    )
492	                )
493	
494	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
495	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
496	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
497	                for h in hypotheses:
498	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
499	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
500	                        raise FirewallViolation(
501	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
502	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
503	                        )
504	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
505	                    trail.record(
506	                        "verdict",
507	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
508	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
509	                        label=verdict.label.value,
510	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
511	                    )
512	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
513	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
514	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
515	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
516	
517	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
518	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
519	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
520	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
521	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
522	            if not claim_records:
523	                raise ValueError(
524	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
525	                )
526	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
527	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
528	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
529	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
530	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
531	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
532	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
533	            run_gate(
534	                GateReview(
535	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
536	                    frm=prev_phase,
537	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
538	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
539	                    payload={
540	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
541	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
542	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
543	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
544	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
545	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
546	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
547	                    },
548	                )
549	            )
550	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
551	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
552	
553	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
554	                    trail.record(
555	                        "preregister",
556	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
557	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
558	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
559	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
560	                    )
561	                    trail.record(
562	                        "verdict",
563	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
564	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
565	                        label=v.label.value,
566	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
567	                    )
568	
569	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
570	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
571	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
572	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
573	                    baseline,
574	                    claim_records,
575	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
576	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
577	                    authority=plan.authority,
578	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
579	                    source_name=defn.name,
580	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
581	                )
582	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
583	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
584	                trail.record(
585	                    "score",
586	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
587	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
588	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
589	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
590	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
591	                )
592	                sc = scorecard
593	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
594	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
595	                track(
596	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
597	                )
598	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
599	                if ar is not None:
600	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
601	
602	        # ---- REPORT ----
603	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
604	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
605	            trail.record(
606	                "report",
607	                "assembled reproducibility package",
608	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
609	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
610	            )
611	
612	        if tuple(visited) != required:
613	            raise FirewallViolation(
614	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
615	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
616	            )
617	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
618	        ok = True
619	    except Exception as exc:
620	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
621	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
622	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
623	        try:
624	            trail.record(
625	                "run_failed",
626	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
627	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
628	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
629	                last_phase=last_phase,
630	            )
631	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
632	            pass
633	        raise
634	    finally:
635	        trace_ctx.close()
636	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
637	            try:
638	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
639	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
640	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
641	                pass
642	
643	    return StudyResult(
644	        study=defn.name,
645	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
646	        baseline=baseline,
647	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
648	        scorecard=scorecard,
649	        phases=tuple(visited),
650	        provenance=trail.entries,
651	        source_version=source_version,
652	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
653	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
654	    )
655

1	"""Adapter registration.
2	
3	A clone registers its :class:`~assay_engine.contracts.study.StudyDefinition` factory under a
4	name; the engine looks it up to run. This is the only place adapters and the engine meet by
5	name, and even here the engine holds only a factory callable — it imports no adapter module
6	itself (ARCHITECTURE.md §3). The instance repository imports the engine, builds its study,
7	and registers it.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import threading
13	from typing import Callable, Dict
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition
16	
17	StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]
18	
19	_REGISTRY: Dict[str, StudyFactory] = {}
20	# Registration is normally a one-shot import-time op, but the check-then-act duplicate guard
21	# is a TOCTOU; a lock makes it atomic if studies are ever registered concurrently (issue #13).
22	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
23	
24	
25	def register_study(name: str, factory: StudyFactory) -> None:
26	    """Register a study factory under ``name``. Raises on duplicate names."""
27	    with _LOCK:
28	        if name in _REGISTRY:
29	            raise ValueError(f"a study named {name!r} is already registered")
30	        _REGISTRY[name] = factory
31	
32	
33	def get_study(name: str) -> StudyDefinition:
34	    """Instantiate the registered study ``name``."""
35	    try:
36	        factory = _REGISTRY[name]
37	    except KeyError:
38	        raise KeyError(f"no study registered under {name!r}") from None
39	    return factory()
40	
41	
42	def registered_studies() -> tuple[str, ...]:
43	    return tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))
44	
45	
46	def clear_registry() -> None:
47	    """Test helper: drop all registrations."""
48	    with _LOCK:
49	        _REGISTRY.clear()
50

1	"""assay_engine — the reusable, dataset-agnostic engine of the hc-assay blueprint.
2	
3	The single architectural rule (ADR-0002, ARCHITECTURE.md §3):
4	
5	    The engine never imports dataset specifics. Adapters implement the interfaces in
6	    ``assay_engine.contracts`` that the engine calls. A clone is: implement the adapter,
7	    register it, run.
8	
9	This package contains only goal-agnostic machinery:
10	
11	- ``contracts``      — the engine ↔ adapter boundary (canonical schema + adapter Protocols).
12	- ``methodology``    — hypotheses, the three verdicts, the two firewalls, the
13	                       measurement↔interpretation fence, confirmatory-test machinery.
14	- ``baseline``       — dataset-agnostic primitives (similarity/distance, descriptive stats) and
15	                       a determinism/reproducibility harness — the raw material; choice-bearing
16	                       builders (embeddings, clustering, graph/topology) are supplied per study by
17	                       the adapter's BaselineBuilder (ADR-0002).
18	- ``orchestration``  — the analysis graph, phase machine, and governance gates.
19	- ``reasoning``      — the tiered LLM reasoning seam.
20	- ``observability``  — self-hosted tracing + experiment tracking.
21	- ``persistence``    — durable checkpointing, data versioning, vector store.
22	- ``provenance``     — the append-only, hash-chained audit trail (GOVERNANCE §3).
23	- ``pipeline``       — ``run_study``: the composed, governed end-to-end runner (ADR-0010).
24	- ``registry``       — adapter registration.
25	
26	Nothing in this package may import an adapter module or name a specific dataset, authority,
27	or taxonomy.
28	"""
29

33	# subpackages (imported lazily there); these names are dependency-free to import.

1	"""Type-faithful canonical hashing — the engine's single content-digest primitive.
2	
3	Two unrelated places need a *stable, type-faithful* content hash: the baseline determinism
4	harness (so the same inputs reproduce the same baseline) and pre-registration (so a lock
5	binds the exact hypothesis content). Both must distinguish values that merely share a
6	``str()`` — ``int 1`` from ``str "1"``, ``date(2020,1,1)`` from ``"2020-01-01"`` — or the
7	hash is a liability rather than a guarantee.
8	
9	This module is the one implementation, hoisted here so it sits *below* both
10	``methodology`` and ``baseline`` in the import graph (neither may depend on the other). It is
11	dependency-free and pure. Anything it cannot canonicalize reproducibly is refused **loudly**:
12	a default (address-based) ``repr`` would make the digest differ every process and silently
13	break the guarantee it exists to provide.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import datetime as _dt
19	import decimal
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import uuid
23	from typing import Any, Mapping
24	
25	
26	def keytag(key: Any) -> str:
27	    """A type-faithful, sortable string for a mapping key, so distinct keys that happen to
28	    share a ``str()`` (e.g. int ``1`` vs str ``"1"``) do NOT collide."""
29	    return f"{type(key).__name__}::{key!r}"
30	
31	
32	def canonical_plain(value: Any) -> Any:
33	    """Canonicalize ``value`` into a fully JSON-native, type-faithful structure for hashing.
34	
35	    Mappings become a sorted list of ``[typed-key, value]`` pairs; bytes, sets, and any
36	    non-JSON-native leaf are tagged with their type rather than stringified. Nothing is coerced
37	    with ``str()``, so the content hash distinguishes values that share a ``str()``.
38	    """
39	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, bool, int, float)):
40	        return value  # JSON-native scalars; json encodes 1, "1", true, 1.0 distinctly
41	    if isinstance(value, bytes):
42	        return {"__bytes__": value.hex()}
43	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
44	        pairs = sorted(
45	            ([keytag(k), canonical_plain(v)] for k, v in value.items()), key=lambda p: p[0]
46	        )
47	        return {"__map__": pairs}
48	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
49	        return [canonical_plain(v) for v in value]
50	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
51	        return {"__set__": sorted((canonical_plain(v) for v in value), key=repr)}
52	    # A small allowlist of leaf types with a value-faithful canonical form.
53	    if isinstance(value, (_dt.date, _dt.datetime, _dt.time)):
54	        return {"__temporal__": value.isoformat()}
55	    if isinstance(value, decimal.Decimal):
56	        return {"__decimal__": str(value)}
57	    if isinstance(value, uuid.UUID):
58	        return {"__uuid__": str(value)}
59	    # Anything else is refused LOUDLY rather than repr-tagged: a default (address-based) repr
60	    # would make the digest differ every process, silently breaking the guarantee.
61	    raise TypeError(
62	        f"cannot canonicalize a leaf of type "
63	        f"{type(value).__module__}.{type(value).__qualname__!r} reproducibly — convert it to a "
64	        "JSON-native value, bytes, date/datetime/Decimal/UUID before hashing"
65	    )
66	
67	
68	def canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:
69	    """The canonical JSON text of ``value`` — stable across processes and key orderings."""
70	    return json.dumps(canonical_plain(value), sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
71	
72	
73	def hash_bytes(data: bytes) -> str:
74	    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
75	
76	
77	def hash_text(text: str) -> str:
78	    return hash_bytes(text.encode("utf-8"))
79	
80	
81	def hash_value(value: Any) -> str:
82	    """Stable, type-faithful content hash of an arbitrary value.
83	
84	    ``canonical_plain`` canonicalizes everything to JSON-native form first, so ``json.dumps``
85	    needs no ``default=`` coercion — anything it still cannot encode is a real bug to surface,
86	    not to silently stringify.
87	    """
88	    return hash_text(canonical_json(value))
89

1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def duck_mapping_items(value: Any) -> list[tuple[Any, Any]] | None:
62	    """Return ``(key, value)`` items if ``value`` is a dict-like NOT registered as a Mapping.
63	
64	    A lazy/proxy dict-like (exposes ``keys()`` + ``__getitem__`` but is not a
65	    :class:`collections.abc.Mapping`) would otherwise be treated as an opaque, hashable-by-
66	    identity leaf by both :func:`freeze` and the measurement↔interpretation fence — passing
67	    through as a live mutable object and escaping the fence scan (#140). Returns ``None`` for
68	    anything that is not such a duck-typed mapping (incl. real Mappings, str/bytes, sequences).
69	    """
70	    if isinstance(value, (str, bytes, bytearray, Mapping)):
71	        return None
72	    keys = getattr(value, "keys", None)
73	    getitem = getattr(value, "__getitem__", None)
74	    if callable(keys) and callable(getitem):
75	        try:
76	            return [(k, value[k]) for k in keys()]
77	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a misbehaving dict-like is not a usable mapping
78	            return None
79	    return None
80	
81	
82	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
83	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
84	
85	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
86	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
87	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
88	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
89	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
90	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
91	    """
92	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
93	        return value
94	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
95	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
96	    duck = duck_mapping_items(value)
97	    if duck is not None:  # lazy/proxy dict-like — freeze it like a mapping, not a leaf (#140)
98	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in duck})
99	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
100	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
101	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
102	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
103	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
104	        return bytes(value)
105	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
106	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
107	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
108	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
109	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
110	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
111	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
112	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
113	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
114	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
115	        )
116	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
117	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
118	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
119	    try:
120	        hash(value)
121	    except TypeError as exc:
122	        raise TypeError(
123	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
124	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
125	        ) from exc
126	    return value
127	
128	
129	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
130	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`."""
131	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
132	
133	
134	def unfreeze(value: Any) -> Any:
135	    """Recursively convert a frozen structure back into plain JSON-serializable containers.
136	
137	    The inverse of :func:`freeze` for serialization: :class:`FrozenDict` → ``dict``,
138	    ``frozenset`` → sorted ``list`` (stable order for reproducible output), ``tuple`` →
139	    ``list``. Leaves are returned unchanged. ``json.dumps`` does not know :class:`FrozenDict`
140	    (it is a Mapping, not a ``dict`` subclass), so an operator serializing a deep-frozen public
141	    payload — e.g. ``GateReview.payload`` — must thaw it first (#141).
142	    """
143	    if isinstance(value, (FrozenDict, Mapping)):
144	        return {k: unfreeze(v) for k, v in value.items()}
145	    if isinstance(value, frozenset):
146	        # sort when elements are mutually orderable; otherwise keep insertion-free stable repr
147	        try:
148	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value)]
149	        except TypeError:
150	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
151	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
152	        return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
153	    return value
154

# Check if there are entries in the lockfile WITHOUT any hash lines following them
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/requirements.lock') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Find package declarations (pkg==version \) not followed by --hash
packages_without_hashes = []
lines = content.split('\n')
i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    line = lines[i].strip()
    if re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+==[0-9]', line):
        # check if next non-empty line has --hash or # via
        j = i + 1
        has_hash = False
        while j < len(lines) and (lines[j].strip().startswith('--hash') or lines[j].strip().startswith('#') or not lines[j].strip()):
            if lines[j].strip().startswith('--hash'):
                has_hash = True
            j += 1
        if not has_hash:
            packages_without_hashes.append(line)
    i += 1
print('Packages without hashes:', packages_without_hashes[:10])
print('Total:', len(packages_without_hashes))
"

content.content:
# Transcripts (provenance)

This directory captures the **Claude session transcripts** behind the work in this repository,
so the provenance and effort of the agent interactions can be reviewed and audited alongside the
code.

## What's here

- `sessions/<session-id>.jsonl` — the main session transcript (one JSON object per line: prompts,
  tool calls, tool results, model output).
- `sessions/<session-id>/subagents/*.jsonl` — transcripts of subagents spawned in that session.
- `sessions/<session-id>/workflows/...` — workflow scripts and their subagent transcripts.
- `MANIFEST.json` — what was captured, when, and a SHA-256 of each file.

## How it's captured

A committed git hook (`.githooks/pre-commit`, enabled via `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`)
runs `scripts/capture_transcripts.py` before every commit, which snapshots the **active** session
(the most-recently-modified transcript) and its subtree into `sessions/`, then stages it. So
**every commit pulls in an up-to-date transcript snapshot.**

To enable the hook on a fresh clone: `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`.

## Scope & caveats (read before publishing)

- Credential-shaped secrets (API keys, OAuth/bearer tokens) are **redacted** on capture.
- Transcripts still contain **local filesystem paths** and may contain the **operator's email**
  and internal reasoning. They are legitimate provenance, but if this repository is made
  **public**, review this directory first — consider scrubbing further or excluding it
  (`git rm -r --cached transcripts/` + a `.gitignore` entry).
- These files are **not** shipped in the built wheel (only `src/assay_engine` is packaged).
- Transcripts are append-mostly JSONL, so they delta-compress well in git history.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: is_unsafe=True, is_metered=True

"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).

A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:

- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
  require stability across resamples.

This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
``indeterminate``).

Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
upper-tail-only implementation.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import datetime as _dt
import math
from typing import Iterable, Sequence

from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
    TimestampAuthority,
    require_preregistered,
)
from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict


def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.

    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
    """
    if authority is not None:
        require_preregistered(
            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
        )
    else:
        require_locked(hypothesis)


def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.

    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
    """
    if not hypothesis.locked:
        raise FirewallViolation(
            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
        )


def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")


def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")


def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
    if not math.isfinite(value):
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")


def verdict_from_pvalue(
    hypothesis_id: str,
    *,
    statistic: float,
    p_value: float,
    alpha: float,
    powered: bool,
    direction_supports_claim: bool,
    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
) -> Verdict:
    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.

    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).

    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
    """
    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
    _validate_alpha(alpha)
    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)

    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
    common = dict(
        statistic=statistic,
        threshold=alpha,
        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
    )
    if not powered:
        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
    if p_value <= alpha:
        if direction_supports_claim:
            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)


def confirm_unit_level(
    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
    *,
    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
    statistic: float,
    p_value: float,
    alpha: float,
    powered: bool,
    direction_supports_claim: bool,
    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
) -> Verdict:
    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.

    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
    """
    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
    return verdict_from_pvalue(
        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
        statistic=statistic,
        p_value=p_value,
        alpha=alpha,
        powered=powered,
        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
    )


def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
    n = len(null)
    if tail == "greater":
        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
    else:
        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)


def _resample_stability(
    resamples: Sequence[float], null: Sequence[float], tail: Direction, alpha: float
) -> float:
    """Fraction of resamples that **reproduce the observed effect**.

    Each resample is scored as its own observed statistic against the null and must itself be
    significant in the predicted tail (empirical p ≤ ``alpha``). This measures reproduction of
    the observed *effect* — not merely landing on the predicted side of the null center, which a
    resample distribution clearing the null median by an epsilon would satisfy while reproducing
    nothing of the observed magnitude (audit pass 2, #139). The closer to 1.0, the more
    consistently the effect's significance reproduces across resamples.
    """
    significant = sum(1 for r in resamples if _empirical_p(null, r, tail) <= alpha)
    return significant / len(resamples)


_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")


def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time
    argument is optional and, if given, must match the pre-registered one.

    The resolved direction is validated against ``{"greater", "less"}`` and any other value is
    rejected: an unrecognized direction would otherwise fall through to the 'less' tail in
    :func:`_empirical_p`, silently flipping supported↔contradicted (audit pass 2, #128).
    """
    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
    if locked_dir is not None:
        if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
            raise ValueError(
                "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
                f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
            )
        resolved = locked_dir
    elif predicted_direction is None:
        raise ValueError(
            "no predicted_direction: set it on the hypothesis at pre-registration "
            "(preferred) or pass it explicitly"
        )
    else:
        resolved = predicted_direction
    if resolved not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS:
        raise ValueError(
            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got {resolved!r} — an "
            "unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict"
        )
    return resolved


def confirm_whole_corpus(
    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
    *,
    observed: float,
    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
    alpha: float,
    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
) -> Verdict:
    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.

    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).

    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:

    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.

    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
    """
    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
    _validate_alpha(alpha)
    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
    if not null_distribution:
        raise ValueError(
            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
        )
    for v in null_distribution:
        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")

    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
    n = len(null_distribution)

    stability: float | None = None
    if resample_statistics is not None:
        if not resample_statistics:
            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
        for r in resample_statistics:
            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, null_distribution, direction, alpha)
    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold

    rule = (
        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
    )
    evidence = {
        "p_support": p_support,
        "p_contradict": p_contra,
        "null_n": n,
        "predicted_direction": direction,
        "stability": stability,
        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
    }
    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)

    # A contradiction (significant in the opposite tail) is reportable without stability.
    if p_contra <= alpha:
        return Verdict.contradicted(
            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
        )
    if stability is None:
        return Verdict.indeterminate(
            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
            rule,
            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
            **common,
        )
    if not stable:
        return Verdict.indeterminate(
            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
        )
    if p_support <= alpha:
        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
    return Verdict.indeterminate(
        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
    )

content.content:
# ADR-0003 — Local-first, data-sovereign; self-hosted observability

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-15)

## Context

The analyzed data may be sensitive, licensed, or otherwise constrained. Reproducibility
also requires that traces and experiment records be retained. SaaS observability (managed
tracing) ships analyzed content off-box and, at corpus scale with long retention, is also
expensive.

## Decision

All engine computation, storage, tracing, experiment tracking, and the **bulk** reasoning tier
run **on-box**. Observability is **self-hosted** (OpenTelemetry → a local collector) plus a
local experiment-tracking store. The bulk reasoning tier uses a local model; no data,
embeddings, traces, or results from these paths leave the machine.

Two distinct concerns must not be conflated:

- **Billing model:** the optional **high-stakes** reasoning tier uses a fixed-cost subscription
  path (the `claude` CLI / Agent SDK with an OAuth token) — *no metered API*. `scrubbed_env()`
  strips metered credentials, off-box redirects (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`), and proxies.
- **Data residency:** "no metered API" is **not** the same as "on-box". The high-stakes tier
  sends prompt content (derived from the data) to a frontier model hosted by Anthropic over the
  network — it is **off-box** by construction (there is no local frontier model). A study whose
  data must never leave the box must use only the bulk/local tier or `UnconfiguredReasoningSeam`.

## Consequences

- SaaS tracing/observability is disqualified by the data-sovereignty constraint,
  independent of cost.
- The engine, storage, observability, and bulk reasoning are runnable fully offline; only the
  optional high-stakes tier depends on a remote service (and is off-box) to advance.
- The operator owns retention and the full reproducibility record locally.

content.content:
"""Governance gates — deterministic, code-enforced transition checks.

A gate guards a transition between phases. It evaluates code-enforced preconditions (the
firewalls, lock state) and, where the governance model requires it, a recorded human
approval. A gate cannot be silently bypassed: a transition is legal only if the gate returns
an ``approved`` decision, and a ``requires_human`` gate refuses to pass unless a provenance
recorder is supplied to capture the decision (audit pass 1, issue #11).

Two complementary gate mechanisms exist (ADR-0010):

- This ``Gate``/``evaluate`` model is the **declarative precondition check** — mode-aware
  transition legality (issue #9) plus a fail-loud human-approval hook that refuses to pass a
  ``requires_human`` gate without a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`-backed
  recorder. It is used by a study that builds its own LangGraph topology with
  :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` for durable interrupt/resume.
- The composed runner :func:`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study` uses the *synchronous*
  ``GateReview``/``GateHandler`` model for in-process governance (a handler may approve, block,
  or be wired to park). Both record every decision to the append-only provenance trail, which
  **is** now wired (:mod:`assay_engine.provenance`) — the present-tense guarantees in
  GOVERNANCE.md hold. Use the graph mechanism when you need cross-process durable parking; use
  the runner handler for a straight-line governed run.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping

from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyMode
from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, legal_transition

ALL_MODES: frozenset[StudyMode] = frozenset(StudyMode)
ProvenanceRecorder = Callable[["GateDecision"], None]


class GateError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when a transition is attempted through a gate that did not approve it."""


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class GateDecision:
    approved: bool
    gate: str
    reason: str
    evidence: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        object.__setattr__(self, "evidence", freeze_mapping(self.evidence))


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Gate:
    """A guard on the transition ``frm -> to``.

    ``precondition`` is a pure, code-enforced check over the run context returning a
    :class:`GateDecision`. ``modes`` scopes which study modes this gate applies to (default:
    the full pipeline). ``requires_human`` marks gates that additionally need a recorded
    operator approval, which is enforced fail-loud at :meth:`evaluate`.
    """

    name: str
    frm: Phase
    to: Phase
    precondition: Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], GateDecision]
    requires_human: bool = False
    modes: frozenset[StudyMode] = ALL_MODES

    def evaluate(
        self,
        context: Mapping[str, Any],
        *,
        study_modes: frozenset[StudyMode] | None = None,
        record: ProvenanceRecorder | None = None,
    ) -> GateDecision:
        # The gate's own `modes` is a self-declaration. When the orchestration layer supplies
        # the running study's actual modes, the transition must be legal under THOSE modes —
        # so a discovery-only run cannot pass a CONFIRM->ADJUDICATE gate even if the gate
        # over-declares its modes (audit pass 2, issue #23). Fall back to self.modes only
        # until the runner is wired.
        effective_modes = self.modes if study_modes is None else (study_modes & self.modes)
        if not legal_transition(self.frm, self.to, effective_modes):
            raise GateError(
                f"gate {self.name!r} declares an illegal transition for the effective modes: "
                f"{self.frm.name} -> {self.to.name}"
            )
        decision = self.precondition(context)
        if not decision.approved:
            raise GateError(f"gate {self.name!r} blocked transition: {decision.reason}")
        if self.requires_human and record is None:
            # Fail loud: a human-in-the-loop gate must not pass without a recorder to
            # capture the approval to the (eventual) append-only provenance trail.
            raise GateError(
                f"gate {self.name!r} requires recorded human approval but no provenance "
                "recorder was supplied"
            )
        if record is not None:
            record(decision)
        return decision

content.content:
"""A minimal, runnable hc-assay study — `python examples/minimal_study.py`.

A study is: implement the adapter Protocols for your dataset, assemble a StudyPlan, and call
run_study. This example uses a tiny synthetic in-memory corpus so it runs with the
dependency-free core install (`pip install assay-engine`) — no backends, no real dataset.

It runs BOTH modes:
  - DISCOVERY: surface a hypothesis on one partition, confirm it on a held-out partition.
  - ADJUDICATE: test an external claim against the blind baseline.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import datetime as _dt
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from assay_engine import (
    ClaimRecord,
    Corpus,
    StudyDefinition,
    StudyMode,
    StudyPlan,
    Unit,
    auto_approve,
    run_study,
)
from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import build_baseline_artifact
from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard, DiscoverConfirmSplit
from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import LocalHmacAuthority, lock_hypothesis
from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict

# A pre-registration authority (on-box HMAC). A real study would persist this secret securely.
AUTHORITY = LocalHmacAuthority(b"example-study-secret-key-00000001")
PAST = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc) - _dt.timedelta(hours=1)


# --- adapter: ingestion (raw source -> canonical corpus) ---
class Parser:
    def parse(self, source: Path) -> Corpus:
        # a real parser reads `source`; here we synthesize 6 units deterministically.
        return Corpus(units=tuple(Unit(f"u{i}", f"control text {i}") for i in range(6)))

    def source_fingerprint(self, source: Path) -> str:
        return "example-source-v1"


# --- adapter: the blind baseline (built from the data only) ---
class BaselineBuilder:
    def build(self, corpus: Corpus, *, claim_guard: ClaimBlindGuard[object]) -> BaselineArtifact:
        mean_len = sum(len(u.text) for u in corpus.units) / len(corpus.units)
        return build_baseline_artifact(
            corpus, {"mean_text_len": mean_len}, component_versions={"builder": "example@1"}
        )


# --- adapter: discovery + confirmation (Firewall B) ---
def discover(discovery_corpus: Corpus) -> list[Hypothesis]:
    h = Hypothesis(
        hypothesis_id="H1", statement="units share above-chance internal similarity",
        kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
        test_name="permutation", decision_rule="empirical p<=0.05 in the 'greater' tail",
        predicted_direction="greater",
    )
    return [lock_hypothesis(h, authority=AUTHORITY, instant=PAST)]


def confirm_held_out(hypothesis: Hypothesis, held_out: Corpus) -> Verdict:
    # a real study computes a statistic on `held_out`; here a deterministic stand-in.
    return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, "held-out test passed")


# --- adapter: external claims (Firewall A) ---
class Claims:
    def claims(self) -> list[ClaimRecord]:
        return [ClaimRecord(claim_id="c1", subject="c1", referents=("c1",),
                            assertion={"expected": "high"})]

    def claim_fingerprint(self) -> str:
        return "example-claims-v1"


def hypothesis_for(claim: ClaimRecord) -> Hypothesis:
    h = Hypothesis(
        hypothesis_id=f"H-{claim.claim_id}", statement="claim holds against the baseline",
        kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, origin=HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM,
        test_name="adjudication", decision_rule="baseline corroborates the claim",
        source_claim_id=claim.claim_id, predicted_direction="greater",
    )
    return lock_hypothesis(h, authority=AUTHORITY, instant=PAST)


def confirm_claim(hypothesis: Hypothesis, baseline: BaselineArtifact, claim: ClaimRecord) -> Verdict:
    return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, "baseline corroborates")


def main() -> None:
    source = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "corpus"  # unused by the synthetic parser
    definition = StudyDefinition(
        name="example-study",
        modes=frozenset(StudyMode),  # both DISCOVERY and ADJUDICATE
        parser=Parser(),
        research_questions=("does the example run end to end?",),
        claims_source=Claims(),
    )
    plan = StudyPlan(
        definition=definition, source=source,
        baseline_builder=BaselineBuilder(), authority=AUTHORITY,
        split=DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"u0", "u1", "u2"}, {"u3", "u4", "u5"}),
        discover=discover, confirm_held_out=confirm_held_out,
        hypothesis_for=hypothesis_for, confirm_claim=confirm_claim,
    )

    # gate_handler is required: auto_approve opts out of human review explicitly. A real run
    # supplies an operator handler that can block/park.
    result = run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve)

    print(f"study: {result.study}")
    print(f"phases: {[p.name for p in result.phases]}")
    print(f"discovery verdicts: {[(v.hypothesis_id, v.label.value) for v in result.discovery_verdicts]}")
    if result.scorecard is not None:
        sc = result.scorecard
        print(f"scorecard: supported={sc.n_supported} contradicted={sc.n_contradicted} "
              f"indeterminate={sc.n_indeterminate} alignment_rate={sc.alignment_rate}")
    print(f"provenance entries: {len(result.provenance)} (chain verified before return)")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
